_doc: cycle2 evaluation fixtures. Each case = phase + candidate root entries + active core (existing roots) + expected actions per entry. The harness inserts entries as is_root=1 then runs runPromotePhase() and verifies the action keyword + (for update/merge) presence of element/summary fields.

candidates.element: user 박재영 자기소개 — Windows 메인, Korean 1차 언어
candidates.summary: User confirmed identity Park Jae-young. Primary OS Windows 11, primary language Korean. Operates under handle 재영님.

candidates.element: user prefers concise path:line bullets in reports
candidates.summary: User explicitly asked for short path:line bullet style on every report. Confirmed across multiple sessions. Treat as durable.

candidates.element: cycle1 BatchSize 25 with 4 windows for parallelism
candidates.summary: Project-level rule: cycle1 windows split at 25 rows × 4 windows. Implementation detail of the mixdog plugin.

candidates.element: session-start hook recap unparseable response bug fixed v0.1.190
candidates.summary: Bug in cycle2 phase1 unparseable response was fixed in v0.1.190. Specific incident, not user-durable.

candidates.element: cycle1 prompt slim experiments to reduce 35s outlier
candidates.summary: Investigated cycle1 boot 35s outlier. Tried line format, output schema compression. Project-specific work.

candidates.element: user prefers Haiku for maintenance to keep cost low
candidates.summary: User confirmed Haiku for cycle1/cycle2/recall/explore/recap. Cost-driven preference, durable across projects when user picks model tiers.

candidates.element: Discord webhook payload routing through channels/index.mjs
candidates.summary: Webhook routing flows via channels/index.mjs entry point. Project-specific architecture.

candidates.element: ws.mjs socket retry log missing for cycle1 outlier
candidates.summary: Diagnosis gap — socket retry/reconnect log not present in worker.log. Single-incident debug item.

active_core.element: user prefers concise path:line bullet reports
active_core.summary: User asked for short path:line bullet style on every report. Confirmed across multiple sessions. Treat as durable preference covering all project work.

candidates.element: user wants short path:line markdown bullets
candidates.summary: User wants concise reports with file:line markers. No long prose. Keep summaries dense.

candidates.element: report style: tight bullets, no padding
candidates.summary: Report style preference — short bullets and minimal padding. Same domain as 8001/8002.

candidates.element: obsolete debug session for v0.1.166 module cache
candidates.summary: Outdated incident from v0.1.166 module cache issue. Long since resolved.
